fields.text: You're about to reset the game. Are you sure?

gameStartRoutine.note: Move customers into the main deck

info.name: Grocery Hurry-Scurry
info.attribution:
Game layout, library image, and scripting by Robin Harn, released to the Public Domain under CC0.

The following graphics are from http://www.freepik.com and are used under the Freepik license: 
* https://www.freepik.com/icon/king-cake_2583991
* https://www.freepik.com/free-vector/fruit-vegetables-background_7316497.htm

All card graphics are from Google Noto Emoji and are used under the SIL Open Font License: https://fonts.google.com/noto/specimen/Noto+Color+Emoji
info.description: Start as many shops as possible, stock them with food before key customers arrive, and make sure to fend off the scavenging fox!
info.similarAwards:
2007 Fairplay À la carte Runner-up
1996 Spiel des Jahres Recommended
1996 Fairplay À la carte Runner-up

label-guide-2p.text:
Cards Used:		0-10
Starting Hand:	13 cards
Magic Number:	7 tricks

label-guide-3p.text:
Cards Used:		0-10
Starting Hand:	13 cards
Magic Number:	4 tricks

label-guide-4p.text:

label-guide-5p.text:

clickRoutine.header: Add this many points to your score:

clickRoutine.note: Sanity check / typo avoidance 1 of 2: only add value if it is an integer

thenRoutine.note: Sanity check / typo avoidance 2 of 2: only add value if it is a three-digit number

label-guide-players.text:
Actions (3 pts per turn):
* Take a card from market (1 pt)
* Take a card from deck (2 pt)
* Open a new shop (1 pt)
* Add inventory to a shop (1 pt)
* Move fox to one of your own shops (1 pt)
* Move fox to next player (2 pt)
* Move fox to next player end-of-turn (0 pt)

Scoring (4th/7th/10th customer):
+ $3 for biggest shop of one type
+ $1 for second-biggest shop
$ x2 if doubler card is in the shop
+ $0 if fox is in the shop

Additional Final Scoring:
- $2 per shop opened that has no inventory
- $1 per remaining card in hand
